element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundational combat mechanics taught through early enemy encounters
full-screen damage attack; meter drains if knocked down, teaching resource management

element_introduction_order.element: Character-specific move sets (Ninja nunchaku, Kamaitachi sickles, Kunoichi blades/swords)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: differentiated character attributes (slow/powerful, quick/weak, balanced) create distinct playstyles
spatial awareness and environmental interaction against clustered enemies
dual threat awareness—enemy and environment simultaneous threat
culmination of stage mechanics; pattern recognition and character-specific strategy

typical_relief_triggers: power meter fully charged for screen-clear attack
continue between stages allows character swap
typical_tension_triggers: power meter drain after being knocked down
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: linear progression with escalating enemy density and hazard complexity; boss gates create narrative tension peaks; unlimited continues reduce resource scarcity tension

difficulty_curve.early_game: foundational combat mechanics introduced gradually; basic enemy types establish attack patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: hazard types introduced alongside increased enemy density; character-specific move mastery required
difficulty_curve.late_game: complex hazard combinations with simultaneous enemy and environmental threats; boss patterns demand full mechanic repertoire
notable_spikes: stage-end boss encounters escalate significantly from standard enemies
environmental hazards (minefields, helicopters) introduce threat types outside combat system
final stages combine maximum enemy density with hazard complexity

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear stage structure with progressive threat escalation
notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 8 stages moves in single horizontal direction toward boss encounter; enemy types and hazards increase in complexity; power meter provides rhythm break for screen-clearing attacks
Stage culminates in single significant boss enemy; encourages character-specific strategy and pattern learning before progression; allows difficulty reset via continue and character swap
Minefields and armed helicopters create passive threat spaces that damage both player and enemies; teaches spatial awareness beyond pure combat mechanics

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (debug features, level select, regional differences, anti-piracy info)
wikipedia - The Ninja Warriors (1994) gameplay mechanics, character attributes, stage structure, reception
